This uses the ETF screener universe, Yahoo enrichment, a 10-year activity minimum, and a creation-time keep rule of SR10Y > 0.5 or AUM > $0.5B, while explicitly keeping EUO, TLT, LQD, HYG, and sector ETFs.

Choose a subset or keep the broader universe, inspect the interactive frontier points chart, and review rounded portfolio weights for the selected universe only. Efficient frontier on Wikipedia.

Optimizer controls

Universe

Leave blank to optimize over the broader ETF universe. Mixed separators are accepted, duplicates are removed automatically, and optional expected returns can be added beside each symbol.

Selected Universe Editor

This editor follows the active universe: the tickers listed in Ticker subset and optional ER, or all filtered ETFs when that field is empty. Leave Applied ER blank to auto-fill it from the AR Lookback horizon.

Method

The generator uses the Nasdaq ETF universe, Yahoo Finance enrichment, a 10-year activity requirement, a creation-time gate of SR10Y > 0.5 or AUM > $0.5B, explicit keeps for EUO/TLT/LQD/HYG and sector ETFs, and similarity pruning. The browser then rebuilds the covariance matrix from embedded monthly returns and solves a long-only capped-weight frontier with projected-gradient optimization.

  • Data is fetched with a 15-year history buffer, rows must have at least 10 years of activity, the optimization defaults to a 10-year lookback, and the HTML embeds only the trailing 15-year monthly return window.
  • Missing monthly returns inside the 15-year window are backfilled with 0% monthly returns so the optimizer runs without NaNs.
  • Expected returns use the annualized arithmetic mean of the selected monthly return window.
  • Volatility and covariance are annualized from monthly return covariance after backfilling.
  • The frontier is generated over a dense grid of return-risk tradeoff values, then reduced to non-dominated portfolios.
  • Displayed weights are rounded with a configurable minimum weight threshold.
